planets.name: Don
planets.summary: Archive cluster around don: 978 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2020-05-06
artifacts.excerpt: I understand why "Don't question people's lived experience" is a good social norm, but it runs into the problem that people are reliably incredibly wrong about their lived experience literally all the time.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: don, know, really, want.

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2020-08-25
artifacts.excerpt:
What if the reason why women do most of the emotional labour is that we don't count it as emotional labour when men do it?

(I was surprisingly reluctant to tweet this even on alt)
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: don, know, really, want.

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2020-12-10
artifacts.excerpt:
I kinda want to popularise a concept of "badass privilege". i.e. problems that you personally don't experience or even notice because you're a badass.

There's no good reason to popularise this concept except that I am a being of pure chaos and it seems funny.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: don, know, really, want.

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2020-07-29
artifacts.excerpt: I don't really understand why everyone doesn't grind problem solving skills constantly. It feels like almost everyone gets to a certain (often quite low) skill level in what is basically a cheat code for life with unbounded upwards growth and then goes "welp, that's good enough"
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: don, know, really, want.

artifacts.title: Good receipt from 2020-05-06

artifacts.title: Lot receipt from 2020-09-12
artifacts.excerpt:
You know how some people are *really* into being geeks and proudly declare they're geeks and are super into things specifically because they're geeky and it's just incredibly cringe, especially if you're also a geek?

I feel like a lot of identity labels work like this.

artifacts.title: Lot receipt from 2020-02-12
artifacts.excerpt:
Things that seem to help me a lot:

2. Do exercise
3. Switch to the Incredibly Virtuous Diet which cuts out carbs and dairy and a bunch of other delicious things.

I hate that this works. I'm glad I have something that works but I hate that it's this.

artifacts.title: Yeah receipt from 2021-03-23
artifacts.excerpt:
An interesting test of people's politics is "Which groups have to be affected by a problem before you care about it?"

Part of why this is interesting is that everybody goes "Oh yeah you're right this *perfectly* captures why my political opponents are in the wrong"

artifacts.title: Yeah receipt from 2020-12-10
artifacts.excerpt: But something like intelligence, attractiveness, and to some degree wealth... yeah, it's pretty great to be hot, smart, and wealthy (or so I'm told...), and modesty and status norms make it hard to acknowledge that during privilege discourse.

artifacts.title: Yeah receipt from 2020-12-01
artifacts.excerpt:
@visakanv @maybegray Yeah. The thing that stops people doing this isn't that they're lazy (cf. https://t.co/tH2wiY5uRA ) it's that it's scary, and treating it as bad and wrong just makes that worse.

Change is possible and can be fun. The fear and shame maintain stasis if you let them.
